browser-specs.name: Browser-specs
browser-specs.description:
This project exports a curated list of technical Web specifications. The list is used in a variety of ways, including:

To crawl specifications with Reffy to generate machine-readable extracts in Webref, which is then used by tools such as ReSpec and Bikeshed to create terminology and reference links between Web specifications.
To validate specification URLs in the browser-compat-data (BCD) and web-features projects.
To complete the list of specifications in the Specref database.
To create reports on test coverage, Web IDL and specification quality in analyzing tools.
browser-specs.description_html:
This project exports a curated list of technical Web specifications. The list is used in a variety of ways, including:

  • To crawl specifications with Reffy to generate machine-readable extracts in Webref, which is then used by tools such as ReSpec and Bikeshed to create terminology and reference links between Web specifications.
  • To validate specification URLs in the browser-compat-data (BCD) and web-features projects.
  • To complete the list of specifications in the Specref database.
  • To create reports on test coverage, Web IDL and specification quality in analyzing tools.

markup-validator.name: W3C Markup Validator
markup-validator.description: The Markup Validator is a free service by W3C that helps check the validity of Web documents. Most Web documents are written using markup languages, such as HTML or XHTML. These languages are defined by technical specifications, which usually include a machine-readable formal grammar (and vocabulary). The act of checking a document against these constraints is called validation, and this is what the Markup Validator does.
markup-validator.description_html: The Markup Validator is a free service by W3C that helps check the validity of Web documents. Most Web documents are written using markup languages, such as HTML or XHTML. These languages are defined by technical specifications, which usually include a machine-readable formal grammar (and vocabulary). The act of checking a document against these constraints is called validation, and this is what the Markup Validator does.

ttml-imsc1.0.1-text-testcontent.name: IMSC-1.0.1_Text_TestContent
ttml-imsc1.0.1-text-testcontent.description: This reel was created to address early IMSC decoding issues by providing manufacturers with a reference IMSC 1.0/1.0.1 text implementation. The included tests are all pertinent to subtitles produced for production content.
ttml-imsc1.0.1-text-testcontent.description_html: This reel was created to address early IMSC decoding issues by providing manufacturers with a reference IMSC 1.0/1.0.1 text implementation. The included tests are all pertinent to subtitles produced for production content.

ttml-imsc1.1-image-testcontent.name: IMSC-1.1_Image_TestContent
ttml-imsc1.1-image-testcontent.description: This reel is is for IMSC 1.1 Image based subtitle testing. The items that are tested are all pertinent to subtitles produced for production content. This has been built to help manufacturers with their IMSC 1.1 Image implementations by providing them with a reference IMSC package.
ttml-imsc1.1-image-testcontent.description_html: This reel is is for IMSC 1.1 Image based subtitle testing. The items that are tested are all pertinent to subtitles produced for production content. This has been built to help manufacturers with their IMSC 1.1 Image implementations by providing them with a reference IMSC package.

ttml-imsc1.1-text-testcontent.name: IMSC-1.1_Text_TestContent
ttml-imsc1.1-text-testcontent.description: This reel is a continuation of the IMSC-1.0.1_Text_TestContent assets. This reel was created with the same scope as the original reel, where the items that are tested are all pertinent to subtitles produced for production content. This version adds numerous additional tests that focus on foreign language rendering, color compositing to different color spaces, and some new IMSC 1.1 attributes. Like the original reel, this has been built to help manufacturers with their IMSC 1.1 Text implementations by providing them with a reference IMSC package.
ttml-imsc1.1-text-testcontent.description_html: This reel is a continuation of the IMSC-1.0.1_Text_TestContent assets. This reel was created with the same scope as the original reel, where the items that are tested are all pertinent to subtitles produced for production content. This version adds numerous additional tests that focus on foreign language rendering, color compositing to different color spaces, and some new IMSC 1.1 attributes. Like the original reel, this has been built to help manufacturers with their IMSC 1.1 Text implementations by providing them with a reference IMSC package.
